title: Grocery Stores Are Engineered to Steal $47 From You
hook: Every inch of your grocery store is a psychological trap — and the average American overspends $47 every single visit because of it.
angle: Reveals four specific supermarket psychology tricks — loss leaders in the back, fake end-cap sales, eye-level placement, and slow ambient music — with measurable dollar impact, then gives the one counter-move that saves $2,400 a year.
audience: Anyone who grocery shops — especially households spending $200+ per week who feel like their cart always ends up fuller than planned.
key_numbers: $2,400 per year in unnecessary grocery spending
38% more spending with slower ambient music
47% of end-cap items aren't actually on sale
